AISastAnalysisParametersAISastAnalysisMo.description:
- AI_SAST_ANALYSIS_MODE_UNSPECIFIED: Options available for peforming AI triage of Sast Findings.
 - AI_SAST_ANALYSIS_MODE_AGENT_FALLBACK: AI_SAST_ANALYSIS_AGENT_ZEROSHOT_CODE_API = 4; // TODO add when ready.

assignees.description: List of assignees for the pull request.

labels.description: List of labels for the pull request.

reviewers.description: List of reviewers for the pull request.

bazel_targets_query.description: query that should be used for bazel scans.

GroupResponseGroupData.description: Information about objects matching the given key.

aggregation_count.description:
Number of objects in group.
This field is always set.

aggregation_uuids.description:
List of UUIDs of the objects in the group.
Only populated if show_aggregation_uuids is set.

aggregation_value.description: Value of the aggregation operation as calculated by the query.

unique_counts.description:
Map of counts for the given unique_count_paths fields.
Only populated if unique_count_paths is set.

unique_values.description:
Map of values for the given unique_value_paths fields.
Only populated if unique_value_paths is set.

RemediationParametersAutomatedPRParamete.description: AutomatedPRParameters holds the settings for automated PRs.

language_parameters.description: Language-specific parameters for the pull request.

max_open_prs.description: Maximum number of open pull requests allowed.

ScanProfileServiceCreateScanProfileBody.description:
ScanProfile represents the scan configuration to use across different
projects.

meta.description: Meta identifies the tool chain object.

propagate.description: Indicates that the object should be visible in the child namespaces.

tenant_meta.description: Tenant related data for the tenant containing the resource.

uuid.description: UUID is a universally unique identifier for the tool chain profile.

object.description:
ScanProfile represents the scan configuration to use across different
projects.

meta.description: Meta identifies the tool chain object.

propagate.description: Indicates that the object should be visible in the child namespaces.

tenant_meta.description: Tenant related data for the tenant containing the resource.

uuid.description: UUID is a universally unique identifier for the tool chain profile.

exporter_uuids.description:
exporter_uuids is the list of exporter objects that will be used for
exports at the end of the scan.

disable_code_summary.description:
disable_code_summary indicates that the code summary part of the
security review should be disabled whether or not there are findings.

user_prompt.description:
user_prompt is the user prompt for the security review scanner.
it is used to customize the security review scanner and give the
ability to the user to provide their own prompt to customize the
security review and the findings that are reported. This is optional.

@type.description:
A URL/resource name that uniquely identifies the type of the serialized
protocol buffer message. This string must contain at least
one "/" character. The last segment of the URL's path must represent
the fully qualified name of the type (as in
`path/google.protobuf.Duration`). The name should be in a canonical form
(e.g., leading "." is not accepted).

In practice, teams usually precompile into the binary all types that they
expect it to use in the context of Any. However, for URLs which use the
scheme `http`, `https`, or no scheme, one can optionally set up a type
server that maps type URLs to message definitions as follows:

* If no scheme is provided, `https` is assumed.
* An HTTP GET on the URL must yield a [google.protobuf.Type][]
  value in binary format, or produce an error.
* Applications are allowed to cache lookup results based on the
  URL, or have them precompiled into a binary to avoid any
  lookup. Therefore, binary compatibility needs to be preserved
  on changes to types. (Use versioned type names to manage
  breaking changes.)

Note: this functionality is not currently available in the official
protobuf release, and it is not used for type URLs beginning with
type.googleapis.com. As of May 2023, there are no widely used type server
implementations and no plans to implement one.

Schemes other than `http`, `https` (or the empty scheme) might be
used with implementation specific semantics.

v1AISastAnalysisParameters.description: Runtime parameters controlling behavior of ai sast analysis.

fp_triage.description: enable false positive triage of sast findings using AI.

mode.description: mode for triage of sast findings using AI.

retriage.description: enable retriage of sast findings using AI.

v1AutomatedScanParameters.description:
AutomatedScanParameters represents the parameters that must be applied during
automated cloud scans to the projects associated with the corresponding
profile.

additional_environment_variables.description:
additional_environment_variables is a list of additional environment
variables that must be used, specific to languages or tools.

bazel_configuration.description:
bazel_configuration is the configuration that should be used for Bazel
builds. If populated, scans are performed as a bazel repository.

call_graph_languages.description:
call_graph_languages is the list of languages that should be used to
calculate call graphs. If empty, default values are used.

disable_code_snippet_storage.description:
disable_code_snippet_storage is a boolean value that indicates if the source code snippet
related to a finding should be stored or displayed. Default will be off.

disable_code_storage.description:
disable_code_storage is a boolean value that indicates if the source code related to a finding
should be stored or displayed. Default will be off.

enable_ai_sast_scan.description: enable_ai_sast_scan is a boolean value that indicates if AI SAST scan should be requested.

enable_automated_pr_scans.description:
enable_automated_pr_scans for this repository will allow the github
app to selectively scan PRs and merges for this repository.
If the project is not part of a github app, this setting will be ingored.

enable_pr_incremental_scan.description: enable_pr_incremental_scan is a boolean value that indicates if the PR incremental scan should be requested.

enable_pr_security_review_scan.description: enable_pr_security_review_scan is a boolean value that indicates if a Security Review scan should be requested.

excluded_paths.description: excluded_paths is a list of paths that should be excluded from the scan.

full_pr_scan.description: full_pr_scan indicates whether a full scan should be enabled during PRs.

full_push_scan.description:
full_push_scan indicates whether a full scan should be enabled during
pushes.

included_paths.description: included_paths is a list of paths that should be included in the scan.

languages.description:
languages is a list of languages that should be scanned. If emtpy
default values are used.

v1CountResponse.description: Response to a list count request.

count.description: Number of objects matching the given list parameters.

v1GroupAggregationValueResponse.description:
GroupAggregationValueResponse returns the value of the aggregation if
requested.

v1GroupResponse.description: Response to a list group request.

v1IndexData.description:
IndexData is used to index the resource for search. It's an internal
object.

search_score.description: search_score is the score of the resource for search. Internal use only.

will_be_deleted_at.description: Time that the resource will be deleted.

next_page_id.description: The UUID of last message retrieved.

next_page_token.description: Pagination token that can be used to retrieve the next page of results.

v1Meta.description: Common fields for all Endor Labs resources.

annotations.description:
Annotations can be used to attach metadata to a resource message.
Annotation values can be small or large, structured or unstructured,
and may include characters not permitted by labels.
The keys may contain alphanumerics, underscores (_), dots (.) and dashes
(-). The values of an annotation must be 16384 bytes or smaller.

create_time.description:
Time the resource was created.

Format: 2017-01-15T01:30:15.01Z
RFC 3339: https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt.

created_by.description:
Name and authentication source of the user who created the object,
for example, ewok@endor.ai@google@api-key.

description.description: Resource description. Must be less than 1024 bytes.

kind.description:
Resource kind, for example, HelloResponse.
Auto-generated using the protobuf message proto.MessageName().Name().

name.description: Resource name. Must be 63 characters or less.

parent_kind.description: Parent object resource kind, for example, Project.

references.description: Map of objects referenced in a query API.

tags.description:
List of tags attached to the resource.
Tags can be used to select objects and to find collections of objects that
satisfy certain conditions. A tag must be 63 characters or less.

update_time.description:
Time the resource was last updated.
Note: Updated on all create/patch/delete operations.

Format: 2017-01-15T01:30:15.01Z
RFC 3339: https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt.

updated_by.description:
Name and authentication source of the last user who updated the object,
for example, vulnerabilityingestor@endor.ai@x509.

upsert_time.description:
Time the resource was last upserted.

Note:
create_time is only set the first time the resource is created.
upsert_time is set every time the resource is upseted.

Format: 2017-01-15T01:30:15.01Z
RFC 3339: https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt.

pip_packages.description: the key is the package name to install through pip.

v1RemediationParameters.description: RemediationParameters holds the settings for remediation actions.

v1ScanProfile.description:
ScanProfile represents the scan configuration to use across different
projects.

meta.description: Meta identifies the tool chain object.

propagate.description: Indicates that the object should be visible in the child namespaces.

uuid.description: UUID is a universally unique identifier for the tool chain profile.

v1ScanProfileSpec.description: Spec is the internal specification of the object.

ai_sast_analysis_parameters.description: ai_sast_analysis_parameters contains parameters to dictate AI Sast Analysis workflow specs.

automated_scan_parameters.description:
automated_scan_parameters is the parameters that must be applied during
cloud scans by Endor Labs. This is optional.

exporter_parameters.description: exporter_parameters contains the parameters required for the exporter.

is_default.description: is_default indicates that the scan profile is the namespace default.

remediation_parameters.description:
remediation_parameters contains the parameters required for remediation
actions.

security_review_scanner_parameters.description:
security_review_scanner_parameters contains the parameters required for
the security review scanner.

v1TenantMeta.description: Tenant related data for the tenant containing the resource.

namespace.description:
Namespaces are a way to organize organizational units into virtual
groupings of resources. Namespaces must be a fully qualified name,
for example, the child namespace of namespace "endor.prod" called "app"
is called "endor.prod.app".

v1ToolChainProfile.description:
ToolChainProfile represents the list of tool chains that need to be
installed.

v1ToolChainVersion.description: ToolChainVersion defines the tool chain's source from where it can be installed.

relative_tool_chain_path.description: relative path of the tool chain in tar ball.

sha256_sum.description: SHA256 Digest of the tool that will be downloaded.

urls.description: http mirros from where the tool can be downloaded.

environment_variables.description: environment_variables are the env variables to be set during toolchain installation.

v1UpdateRequest.description: Message used for all update requests.

force.description:
Force will force the update of the resource if any
checks fail.

update_mask.description: Fields to update. Defaults to all fields.
